projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

needs_attention.title: Contact installers for quotes

observations.summary: Steve is walking through the side passage towards the backyard. A dog is following him, and a child is visible in the backyard area near the trampoline.

The front door camera is currently non-functional and is displaying an error message instead of video. Audio sensors indicate activity in the backyard and dog run with dogs barking, and lights are on in the kitchen and downstairs hallway.
Steve is in the kitchen area, initially looking at something on the counter before walking through the hallway. The house is active with movement sounds and a dog barking at the entryway.
Steve is in the kitchen area, leaning over the counter and looking down at something. The house is active with some speech and dog barking detected in the area.
No water flow. Daily usage: 776.4 gal.
The dog_run camera is experiencing a technical failure, displaying error messages and significant visual distortion. No activity in the dog run can be observed due to the loss of video stream.
The side passage and backyard area are empty and still. A ball rests on the trampoline, but no people or animals are visible.
No water flow. Daily usage: 776.4 gal.
Steve is walking through the kitchen and living area, while Penelope is also present in the kitchen. The house is active with music playing in the background.
Steve is moving through the kitchen and living room area. He is seen drinking from a green can as he walks through the room.
Light use at 1.84 gal/min. Daily total: 776.4 gal.

The 'front_yard' camera is not transmitting video and is displaying an error message indicating that no frames have been received. This suggests a technical malfunction or a loss of signal from the camera.
Active use at 2.48 gal/min. Daily total: 742.9 gal.

The front door camera is non-functional and is not receiving any video frames, displaying an error message instead. Audio sensors indicate activity in the backyard and dog run, with dogs barking in the living room.
Low flow at 0.21 gal/min. Daily total: 701.8 gal.
The entryway is currently empty and no people or animals are visible in the frame. Although audio indicates dog barking at the entryway, the visual scene remains still.
The dog run is empty, with a tennis ball and a small red ball resting on the artificial grass. No dogs or people are visible in the camera's view.
The side passage and backyard area are empty and still. A ball is resting on the trampoline, but no people or animals are visible.
No water flow. Daily usage: 699.3 gal.
A dog is walking through the entryway near the staircase. The rest of the house appears quiet.
The living room is empty and quiet, with only the sound of the TV playing in the background. The kitchen and hallway area are visible but unoccupied.
Steve walks from the room beyond the glass doors into the entryway. The entryway was previously empty.
Low flow at 0.17 gal/min. Daily total: 699.3 gal.
Steve and Penelope are moving through the kitchen and hallway area of the house.

insights.summary: Unusually high activity at living_room this hour: 8 observations vs typical 1 for weekday 19:00
Unusually high activity at entryway this hour: 6 observations vs typical 1 for weekday 19:00
Dogs barking repeatedly at living room (4x since 6:05PM).
Dogs barking repeatedly at front door (3x since 6:13PM).
Dogs barking repeatedly at living room (2x since 6:16PM).
[doorbell] The front door camera is not receiving video frames.
[dog_run] The dog_run camera is malfunctioning, showing error messages and heavy visual artifacts.
[front_yard] The front_yard camera is currently not transmitting video, which may indicate a technical issue or loss of connectivity.

There is a concerning confluence of technical failures (front door and front yard cameras are non-functional) and persistent, unclassified audio agitation (impact sounds, unclassified audio, and repeated dog barking) concentrated in the backyard and dog run since 18:18. This suggests the home is experiencing a significant, unvisualized disturbance or external activity that is causing distress to the dogs and the environment, requiring immediate manual check of the perimeter.
Dogs barking repeatedly at front door (2x since 5:09PM).
